python3 -c "
import os
import subprocess
import tempfile

# Try to get duration using soxi (part of sox)
audio_file = '/app/audio.mp3'
try:
    # soxi should give us info about the audio
    result = subprocess.run(['soxi', audio_file], capture_output=True, text=True)
    print('soxi output:')
    print(result.stdout)
    if result.stderr:
        print('stderr:', result.stderr)
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error with soxi: {e}')
    
    # Try a different approach - use Python's wave module on converted file
    try:
        import wave
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.wav') as tmp:
            # Try to convert with sox
            subprocess.run(['sox', audio_file, tmp.name], capture_output=True)
            with wave.open(tmp.name, 'rb') as wav:
                print(f'Channels: {wav.getnchannels()}')
                print(f'Sample width: {wav.getsampwidth()}')
                print(f'Frame rate: {wav.getframerate()}')
                print(f'Frames: {wav.getnframes()}')
                print(f'Duration: {wav.getnframes() / wav.getframerate():.2f} seconds')
    except Exception as e2:
        print(f'Error with wave: {e2}')
"
